name: Jimmy Knepper
abstract: James Minter Knepper (November 22, 1927 – June 14, 2003) was an American jazz trombonist. In addition to his own recordings as leader, Knepper performed and recorded with Charlie Barnet, Woody Herman, Claude Thornhill, Stan Kenton, Benny Goodman, Gil Evans, Thad Jones and Mel Lewis, Toshiko Akiyoshi and Lew Tabackin, and, most famously, …

concerts.label: Miles Davis with Gil Evans and Orchestra
20th Century Innovations: Recent Developments in Jazz
Newport Jazz Festival: Charles Mingus Sextet with Azucena Y Edo Flamenco Dance Group
Kool-Newport Jazz Festival: Tribute to Charlie Parker

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: I was involved with something called the American Jazz Orchestra... And this was a band comprised of mostly big band veterans: ... Jimmy Knepper
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: Jimmy Knepper and Jerry Dodgion had a room together... We all had rooms with balconies... Jimmy Knepper and Jerry Dodgion had a room together and they threw a party.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: There’s another one called Live At Laren in Holland from seventy-nine with a New York band. Red Rodney, John Eckert, Jimmy Knepper, Sam Burtis, Ronnie Cuber, Bennie Aranov, Ray Drummond, and Billy Hart.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Powell
about_quotes.evidence: Mingus had a reputation for — well first of all one of the things that didn’t sit too well with me was the reputation that he had for knocking out Jimmy Knepper’s two front teeth.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Powell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 16, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: John Beal
about_quotes.evidence: This one record I played on it with Gene Roland arrangements, it was Jimmy Knepper’s album and Al and Zoot were on it and Clark Terry, Snooky Young.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Beal interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 18, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Reggie Watkins
about_quotes.evidence: I had only known Jimmy — I never had a Jimmy Knepper record... so it brought me really close to him as an artist.
about_quotes.doc_title: Reggie Watkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 22, 2018
